text.title: Survey Examples and Agentic Gotchas
text.summary: Two worked survey examples (SaaS pricing, feature prioritisation) and agentic failure modes specific to LLM-assisted survey design.

example.note: Pricing survey for SaaS (n=150). Objective: determine acceptable price range for a new tier.
example.code:
Screener: "Do you currently use project management software?" (Yes → continue, No → end) Q1: "How much do you pay monthly for PM tools?" [open numeric] Q2-Q5: Van Westendorp (too cheap, cheap, expensive, too expensive) [open numeric x4] Q6: "Which features would you pay extra for?" [multi-select] Q7: "What would make a $49/mo plan worth it?" [open text]
Results (n=150): - Optimal price: $29-39/month (Van Westendorp OPP) - Must-have features: integrations, mobile app - Insight: 85% expect annual discount option

example.note: Feature prioritisation (n=200). Objective: decide next quarter's roadmap.
example.code:
Q1-Q3: MaxDiff (3 rounds, 4 features per round, balanced incomplete block design) Q4: Satisfaction matrix (current features, 1-5 scale) Q5: "What is the #1 thing you wish we would build?" [open text]
Results (n=200): - Top priority: API access (38% best-of-set across MaxDiff rounds) - Highest gap: Reporting (low satisfaction + high importance) - Surprise: Mobile app less important than assumed

antipattern.description: LLM drafts a Likert 5 scale by default for every attitude question.
antipattern.reason: Use 7-point for nuanced attitude measurement where you need to detect small shifts; binary for behavioral self-report where ambiguity is the failure mode.

antipattern.description: Agent summarises open-end responses with top-line themes and drops disconfirming quotes.
antipattern.reason: Require quote-with-respondent-ID grounding for every theme claim; disconfirming quotes are the most valuable signal in qualitative open-ends.

antipattern.description: Agent generates MaxDiff with unbalanced item-to-set assignment.
antipattern.reason: MaxDiff requires balanced incomplete block design; unbalanced assignment produces biased utilities. Use Conjointly or Sawtooth, not agent-generated task lists.

antipattern.description: Agent reports "NPS went up 3 points" without acknowledging the ±5-7 confidence interval.
antipattern.reason: Gate any NPS reporting through a stats checker tool; LLM-only NPS commentary is almost always underpowered and overconfident.

see-also.link: Question bank for pricing, satisfaction, feature priority
